heading: Limitation on State income taxation of certain pension income
label: 114 Limitation on State income taxation of certain pension income
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), (4), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.Section 514 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, referred to in subsec. (e), is classified to , Labor.section 1144 of Title 29

children.label: a
children.text: No State may impose an income tax on any retirement income of an individual who is not a resident or domiciliary of such State (as determined under the laws of such State).

children.label: A
children.text: a qualified trust under section 401(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 that is exempt under section 501(a) from taxation;

children.label: B
children.text: a simplified employee pension as defined in section 408(k) of such Code;

children.label: C
children.text: an annuity plan described in section 403(a) of such Code;

children.label: D
children.text: an annuity contract described in section 403(b) of such Code;

children.label: E
children.text: an individual retirement plan described in section 7701(a)(37) of such Code;

children.label: F
children.text: an eligible deferred compensation plan (as defined in section 457 of such Code);

children.label: G
children.text: a governmental plan (as defined in section 414(d) of such Code);

children.label: H
children.text: a trust described in section 501(c)(18) of such Code; or

children.label: I
children.text: the life or life expectancy of the recipient (or the joint lives or joint life expectancies of the recipient and the designated beneficiary of the recipient), or

children.label: II
children.text: a period of not less than 10 years, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a payment received after termination of employment and under a plan, program, or arrangement (to which such employment relates) maintained solely for the purpose of providing retirement benefits for employees in excess of the limitations imposed by 1 or more of sections 401(a)(17), 401(k), 401(m), 402(g), 403(b), 408(k), or 415 of such Code or any other limitation on contributions or benefits in such Code on plans to which any of such sections apply.
The term “income tax” has the meaning given such term by section 110(c).
The term “State” includes any political subdivision of a State, the District of Columbia, and the possessions of the United States.

For purposes of this section, the term “retired partner” is an individual who is described as a partner in section 7701(a)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and who is retired under such individual’s partnership agreement.

children.label: e
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as having any effect on the application of section 514 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.1So in original. No subsecs. (c) and (d) have been enacted.1
